exception_info.exception_message:
Command failed (exit 143): pochi --model zai/glm-5.1 --max-steps 200 --max-retries 10 --blobs-dir /logs/agent/pochi/blobs --experimental-stream-trajectory /logs/agent/pochi/trajectory.jsonl > >(tee /logs/agent/pochi/stdout.txt) 2> >(tee /logs/agent/pochi/stderr.txt >&2) <<'EOF'
# Liveblocks Collaborative Form (Storage-backed Inputs)

## Background
You are extending a Next.js (App Router) starter that wires up a Liveblocks `RoomProvider` for a single shared collaborative form. The Storage root is intended to be a `LiveObject` with three text fields — `name`, `email`, `message` — plus a `lastUpdate` ISO timestamp. Every keystroke from any connected client must propagate to all other clients through Liveblocks Storage, and the page must show a small badge with the latest ISO timestamp of the most recent write.

The starter scaffolding already pre-installs Node 20, Next.js 14, and the Liveblocks v2 React + Node SDKs. The Liveblocks auth endpoint and `liveblocks.config.ts` types are in place. **The page itself is intentionally not yet wired to Storage** — it currently only renders the static inputs and you must complete the Storage integration.

The app MUST be wired to the real Liveblocks cloud (no mocks, no fake WebSocket, no in-memory stub).

## Requirements
- The collaborative form lives at the route `/` (the App Router root page). All connected clients must join the same Liveblocks room.
- Storage layout (must match exactly): `Storage.form: LiveObject<{ name: string; email: string; message: string; lastUpdate: string }>`.
- The page MUST be a Client Component (or wrap one) since Liveblocks hooks require the client runtime.
- The `RoomProvider` is declared with `initialStorage={{ form: new LiveObject({ name: "", email: "", message: "", lastUpdate: "" }) }}` so a first-visit room starts with empty fields.
- All three inputs must be **two-way bound** to Storage: their visible value is read from `useStorage((root) => root.form.<field>)`, and any user typing into the inputs must call a `useMutation` that writes the new value back to the same `LiveObject` field. Every write must also update `form.lastUpdate` to a fresh `new Date().toISOString()` value.
- The page must render a `<span id="form-last-update">` containing the current `lastUpdate` ISO string from Storage.
- All Liveblocks access must go through the official `@liveblocks/react` provider configured with a server-side authentication endpoint at `/api/liveblocks-auth` that uses `LIVEBLOCKS_SECRET_KEY` and `@liveblocks/node`. `NEXT_PUBLIC_LIVEBLOCKS_PUBLIC_KEY` may also be referenced.
- Use Liveblocks **v2** SDKs (`@liveblocks/client` and `@liveblocks/react` major version 2). Use `LiveObject` for Storage, not bare JS objects.
- The room id MUST be derived from `NEXT_PUBLIC_ZEALT_RUN_ID` at build/runtime. Read the run id from the `ZEALT_RUN_ID` environment variable, re-export it as `NEXT_PUBLIC_ZEALT_RUN_ID=$ZEALT_RUN_ID` BEFORE running `npm run build` and `npm run start`, and use it inside the client as the room id: `form-storage-${NEXT_PUBLIC_ZEALT_RUN_ID}`. (The verifier ALWAYS starts the server itself, so make sure you do **NOT** leave a development server running.)
- **You MUST kill any dev server** (`npm run dev`, `next dev`, background `npm run start`) that you started during implementation before signaling completion. The verifier launches its own production server on port `3000`; a leftover process will conflict with it.

## Implementation Hints
- Use `useStorage((root) => root.form.name)` (and equivalents) to read each field; use `useMutation` to write back into the `LiveObject` via `storage.get("form").set("name", value)`.
- A single `useMutation` that takes `(field, value)` is enough to update any of the three text fields plus `lastUpdate`.
- For Next.js App Router, wrap the page with a `"use client"` boundary; use `@liveblocks/react/suspense` and `ClientSideSuspense` while Storage loads.
- The Liveblocks v2 React SDK uses global types via `declare global { interface Liveblocks { Storage: ... } }`, which is already declared for you in `liveblocks.config.ts`.
- In the auth route, use `new Liveblocks({ secret }).prepareSession(...).allow(room, session.FULL_ACCESS)` and return the body from `session.authorize()` — this is the v2 server SDK API.
- Do **not** add `NEXT_PUBLIC_ZEALT_RUN_ID` or `ZEALT_RUN_ID` to `liveblocks.config.ts`. The room id wiring belongs in the page / room provider.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Project path: `/home/user/project`
- Start command: `npm run start` (after `npm run build`). The verifier sets `NEXT_PUBLIC_ZEALT_RUN_ID=$ZEALT_RUN_ID` before running both `build` and `start`.
- Port: `3000`
- Routes:
  - `GET /` returns the collaborative form HTML.
  - `POST /api/liveblocks-auth` returns HTTP 200 with a non-empty JSON body containing a `token` field minted by `@liveblocks/node` against the real Liveblocks API. Two consecutive calls must return different tokens.
- DOM contract (these are the only stable selectors used by verification):
  - `<input id="form-name">` — collaborative name text input.
  - `<input id="form-email">` — collaborative email text input.
  - `<textarea id="form-message">` — collaborative message textarea.
  - `<span id="form-last-update">` — shows the current `lastUpdate` ISO string from Storage. Empty string is allowed before the first edit.
- Behavior (verified across two browser contexts sharing the same room):
  - Any keystroke by context A in any field is reflected in the same field on context B within a few seconds.
  - The reverse holds: edits by B are seen by A.
  - After at least one edit, `#form-last-update` contains a value that parses as ISO-8601 (e.g. matches `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS(.sss)?Z` or equivalent ISO format).
  - When context A reloads the page after both clients disconnect, the `name`/`email`/`message` it sees on first paint match the last persisted values — i.e. Storage is durable.
- No mocks: `@liveblocks/client`, `@liveblocks/react`, and `@liveblocks/node` must remain real dependencies. There must be no `jest.mock(...)`/`vi.mock(...)` of any `@liveblocks/*` module anywhere under the project source.
- Real Liveblocks cloud only — no fake WebSocket, no shadowed packages under `node_modules/@liveblocks/*`.


EOF
